page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.large is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs (2 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core) and 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. It does not include bundled local storage or GPU hardware, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category. However, multi-core and memory-bound metrics are limited. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth (both read and write), and Passmark CPU scores rank in the bottom 10% of benchmarks. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, which assist with specific vector and neural network operations despite the overall low resource density.

Given its hardware profile, this instance offers an entry-level compute option. The lack of local storage and low memory bandwidth make it less suitable for heavy database operations, as evidenced by its low Redis SET and database benchmark scores. It is best positioned for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic web serving, or small-scale utility tasks that require dedicated CPU allocation but do not demand high memory capacity or intensive multi-threaded processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.large is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at 3.3 GHz. Utilizing an x86_64 architecture, this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU performance, though multi-core execution and memory bandwidth fall into the lower percentiles. The processor supports advanced vector extensions such as AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI. This instance is qualitatively positioned as a budget-friendly, entry-level compute option. It is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and small-scale utility workloads that benefit from dedicated CPU resources without requiring high memory density or intensive multi-threaded performance.
